metadata.title: The Criminality Detector
metadata.notes: Calling Bullshit (Vox Style), film seven. Source verified: 'In late 2016… Automated Inference on Criminality Using Face Images' on arXiv; Lombroso passage confirmed. NAMING: transcript garbles the authors — narration says 'a paper'; description carries the corrected citation (Xiaolin Wu & Xi Zhang, arXiv 2016). VISUAL SAFETY RULE: no photoreal faces anywhere — the face is always a schematic ink line-drawing; photo sources drawn as stacks, never people. Accuracy claim spoken as 'startling accuracy' (transcript says 'high accuracy'; ~90% figure not verified in source — kept off narration). Color law: crimson = the bias entering, navy = the three measurements. Outro pads A11.
chapters: Where did the photos come from?

beats.narration_text: In late twenty-sixteen, a paper appeared online claiming an algorithm could pick criminals out of ordinary headshots with startling accuracy.
beats.tts_normalized_text: In late twenty-sixteen, a paper appeared online claiming an algorithm could pick criminals out of ordinary headshots with startling accuracy.

shot.note: cold open — archive box of photographs, ALL face-down, one edge-lit; no faces visible

beats.narration_text: The scary part isn't that it seemed to work. It's why.
beats.tts_normalized_text: The scary part isn't that it seemed to work. It's why.

beats.narration_text: The idea is old: an eighteen-seventies doctor measured criminals' skulls, hunting for the face of crime. It was pseudoscience then.
beats.tts_normalized_text: The idea is old: an eighteen-seventies doctor measured criminals' skulls, hunting for the face of crime. It was pseudoscience then.

shot.note: antique brass calipers and a phrenology-style diagram, objects only, no people

beats.narration_text: The modern version: feed a machine eleven hundred ordinary headshots and seven hundred convict photos, and let it find the difference.
beats.tts_normalized_text: The modern version: feed a machine eleven hundred ordinary headshots and seven hundred convict photos, and let it find the difference.

viz.note: data → box → verdicts flow; two drawn photo-stacks feed the box (counts per the source's description; stacks, never faces)

beats.narration_text: It found one. Three measurements, actually: eye-corner distance, nose-to-mouth angle, upper-lip curvature.
beats.tts_normalized_text: It found one. Three measurements, actually: eye-corner distance, nose-to-mouth angle, upper-lip curvature.

viz.note: schematic ink line-face; three navy measurement lines with serif labels

beats.narration_text: Before you believe a black box, interrogate what went in.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Before you believe a black box, interrogate what went in.

card.title: WHERE DID THE PHOTOS COME FROM?

beats.narration_text: The non-criminal photos were professional headshots — people at their best, faintly smiling. The convict photos were official ID documents. Nobody smiles for a government ID.
beats.tts_normalized_text: The non-criminal photos were professional headshots — people at their best, faintly smiling. The convict photos were official ID documents. Nobody smiles for a government ID.

viz.note: two labeled stacks: 'professional headshots' vs 'official ID documents' — source explicitly says NOT mug shots; source chips, no faces

beats.narration_text: Watch the three criminal features as a face relaxes from a slight smile to a flat stare: all three shift together.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Watch the three criminal features as a face relaxes from a slight smile to a flat stare: all three shift together.

viz.note: THE beat: the schematic face morphs smile → flat; all three navy measurement lines visibly move together

beats.narration_text: The algorithm never found the face of crime. It found the face of a professional photo shoot.
beats.tts_normalized_text: The algorithm never found the face of crime. It found the face of a professional photo shoot.

beats.narration_text: You never needed to understand the algorithm. Interrogate the inputs and the outputs; the box in between can stay closed.
beats.tts_normalized_text: You never needed to understand the algorithm. Interrogate the inputs and the outputs; the box in between can stay closed.

viz.note: crimson bias stream flows through an untouched ink box into the verdicts; the box never opens

beats.narration_text: So when an AI's claim sounds like science fiction, don't audit the math. Audit the training data.
beats.tts_normalized_text: So when an AI's claim sounds like science fiction, don't audit the math. Audit the training data.
